UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user registered on the QarWheels platform, holding their basic profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and potentially login. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Car)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Car.title: Car
Car.description: Represents a vehicle owned by a user, including its core details, VIN information, and current status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Car entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this car. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Car)

vin.description: Vehicle Identification Number, a globally unique identifier for the car. Used for prefilling car information via external APIs.

model.description: The specific model of the car (e.g., 'Camry', 'X5').

year.description: The manufacturing year of the car.

color.description: The exterior color of the car.

currentMileage.description: The current recorded mileage of the car.

lastMileageUpdateDate.description: The date and time when the current mileage was last updated.

purchaseDate.description: The date when the user acquired the car.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the car, typically stored in Firebase Storage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the car record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the car record was last updated.

Vendor.title: Vendor
Vendor.description: Represents a service provider or parts store registered on the QarWheels platform, including their location and approval status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vendor entity.

name.description: The official name of the garage or parts store.

description.description: A brief description of the vendor's services or offerings.

address.description: The physical street address of the vendor's location.

city.description: The city where the vendor is located.

country.description: The country where the vendor is located (e.g., 'Qatar').

phoneNumber.description: The main contact phone number for the vendor.

email.description: The main contact email address for the vendor.

websiteUrl.description: The official website URL for the vendor.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the vendor's location, for map display.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the vendor's location, for map display.

status.description: The approval status of the vendor ('Pending Approval', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'Active', 'Inactive').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the vendor record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vendor record was last updated.

ServiceRecord.title: ServiceRecord
ServiceRecord.description: Details of a historical service performed on a specific car, including vendor information and invoice links.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceRecord entity.

carId.description: Reference to the Car that received the service. (Relationship: Car 1:N ServiceRecord)

vendorId.description: Reference to the Vendor who performed the service. (Relationship: Vendor 1:N ServiceRecord)

serviceType.description: The general category of service performed (e.g., 'Oil Change', 'Brake Service', 'Tire Rotation').

serviceDescription.description: A detailed description of the work performed during the service.

serviceDate.description: The date and time when the service was completed.

mileageAtService.description: The car's mileage at the time the service was performed.

cost.description: The total cost of the service.

invoiceUrl.description: URL to the invoice document for this service, stored in Firebase Storage.

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments about the service.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the service record was last updated.

ServicePrediction.title: ServicePrediction
ServicePrediction.description: An AI-generated prediction for future car maintenance needs, linked to a specific car.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServicePrediction entity.

carId.description: Reference to the Car for which the prediction was made. (Relationship: Car 1:N ServicePrediction)

predictedServiceType.description: The type of service predicted (e.g., 'Oil Change', 'Tire Replacement', 'Brake Inspection').

predictedDueDate.description: The estimated date when the predicted service will be due.

predictedMileage.description: The estimated mileage at which the predicted service will be due.

reasoning.description: A detailed explanation from the AI model about why this service is predicted.

confidenceScore.description: A numerical score (0.0-1.0) indicating the AI model's confidence in this prediction.

predictionTimestamp.description: The date and time when this service prediction was generated.

status.description: The current status of the prediction (e.g., 'Open', 'Actioned', 'Dismissed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service prediction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the service prediction record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches '{userId}'. The 'id' field within the document must match the '{userId}' path parameter for data integrity.

Stores details for each car owned by a user. This is a subcollection of a user's profile. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring the car belongs to the user defined by the path.

Contains historical service records for a specific car. Nested under a user's car, ensuring ownership is tied to the user and car. Includes denormalized 'carId' for authorization independence within the subcollection context.
Stores AI-generated service predictions for a specific car. Nested under a user's car. Includes denormalized 'carId' for authorization independence within the subcollection context.

Stores information about service providers and parts stores. Includes 'ownerId' (denormalized user ID of the vendor registrant) for vendor-specific access and a 'status' field ('Approved', 'Pending Approval') for controlling public visibility. Public users can only read 'Approved' vendors, requiring explicit client-side query filtering enforced by security rules. Admins can manage all vendors regardless of status.

A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path where '{adminId}' matches a user's UID grants administrative privileges to that user. Document content can be minimal or empty.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure for QarWheels prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data (profiles, cars, service records, and service predictions) are nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This path-based ownership makes security rules extremely simple and atomic: access to any document within a user's branch can be authorized by checking `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent documents, thus ensuring Authorization Independence.

For `Car` documents, the `userId` is explicitly denormalized into the document, reinforcing ownership and allowing for future flexibility if `Car` documents were ever to be queried or moved to a top-level collection. Similarly, `ServiceRecord` and `ServicePrediction` documents contain `carId`, linking them directly to their parent car, while their parent `userId` is derived from the path.

Vendor data, which has both public and private/admin-managed states, is handled within a single `/vendors/{vendorId}` collection. To achieve QAPs (Rules are not Filters) for public listing, each `Vendor` document includes an `ownerId` (the user who registered the vendor) for management by the vendor themselves, and a `status` field. Public read access will require client queries to explicitly filter for `status == 'Approved'`, with security rules enforcing this query constraint. This ensures that a `list` operation will only ever return approved vendors, satisfying the QAP principle without requiring the rules to act as filters. Admin roles are managed via a dedicated `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection, using the 'existence over content' pattern for DBAC.

Overall, this design supports robust `list` operations by structuring data hierarchically for user-owned content and by using explicit status fields and rule-enforced query parameters for shared, global data. Authorization is explicit, self-contained within the document or its immediate path, and avoids complex hierarchical lookups.
